news.title:
Middle East tensions may disrupt India's $9B auto exports, affecting shipping routes like Strait of Hormuz.
Maruti Suzuki & Hyundai face higher freight costs; Gulf market key for 25% of India's vehicle exports.
news.summary:
Middle East tensions may disrupt India's $9B auto exports, affecting shipping routes like Strait of Hormuz.
Maruti Suzuki & Hyundai face higher freight costs; Gulf market key for 25% of India's vehicle exports.

news.title:
Middle East tensions may impact Indian auto stocks like Maruti Suzuki, Tata Motors, Hyundai Motor, Ashok Leyland, and Bajaj Auto.
Geopolitical risks threaten Gulf shipping routes, raising costs and weakening order visibility for key export-oriented automakers.
news.summary:

news.title: Hyundai Motor India Ltd announces a change in management under Regulation 30 (LODR).
news.summary: Hyundai Motor India Ltd announces a change in management under Regulation 30 (LODR).

news.title:
Hyundai Motor Investment Inc seeks reclassification from promoter to public category for Hyundai Motor India Ltd.
The application, based on SEBI Listing Regulations 2015, was submitted on Feb 6, 2026.
news.summary:
Hyundai Motor Investment Inc seeks reclassification from promoter to public category for Hyundai Motor India Ltd.

news.title:
Hyundai Motor India Ltd. will participate in investor meetings from Feb 9-11, 2026.
Meetings are organized by Nuvama Institutional Equities and Axis Capital in Mumbai.
news.summary:

news.title:
Textile capex hits 14-year high with Rs 44,019 crore projects by 2025.
Investments surged before the India-US deal; CMIE data highlights growth.
news.summary:

news.title:
Hyundai Motor India reports Q3 margin impact due to rising commodity prices.
Pune plant costs add to the pressure, affecting margins by 100 bps for a year.
news.summary:

news.title:
Hyundai Motor India reported Q3 and 9-month results with nearly 80K new car bookings.
The company marked its 10th year as market leader and started Pune plant production.
news.summary:

news.title:
Hyundai Motor India's Q3 EBITDA rose to ₹20.2B from ₹18.8B YoY.
EBITDA margin slightly declined to 11.2% from 11.3% YoY.
news.summary:
Hyundai Motor India's Q3 EBITDA rose to ₹20.2B from ₹18.8B YoY.
EBITDA margin slightly declined to 11.2% from 11.3% YoY.

news.title:
111 firms, including Adani Enterprises, NMDC, and Bajaj Finance, to release Q3FY26 results on Feb 3.
Key names include Adani Ports, Varun Beverages, Pidilite, Solar Industries, and Mankind Pharma.

news.title:
Hyundai Motor India Ltd reported Q3 2025 sales of INR 176,178.1M, up from INR 163,230.9M YoY.
The report also covered earnings for the nine months ending December 31, 2025.
news.summary:

Tata Motors and Hyundai reported strong sales growth in January 2026.
The sales surge was confirmed through the companies' regulatory filings.

Maruti Suzuki flags rising commodity prices as a key factor in margin compression.

news.title:
Hyundai Motors' Q4 results show 5% retail growth in India.
Wholesale growth stood at 0.4%, with 20.3% rise in market demand.
news.summary:

news.title:
Hyundai Motor India Ltd's board meeting on Feb 2, 2026, will review Q3 and nine-month unaudited financial results.
Trading window remains closed from Jan 1, 2026, until 48 hours post financial results declaration.
news.summary:

news.title:
Hyundai Motor India Ltd received a request from Hyundai Motor Investment Inc for reclassification from 'Promoter Group' to 'Public'.
The request will be reviewed by the Board, with no impact on the promoter group's overall shareholding.
news.summary:
Hyundai Motor India Ltd received a request from Hyundai Motor Investment Inc for reclassification from 'Promoter Group' to 'Public'.
The request will be reviewed by the Board, with no impact on the promoter group's overall shareholding.

news.title:
Hyundai Motor's December sales rose to 58,702 units from 55,078 units last year.
Sales missed the estimate of 63,030 units despite showing year-on-year growth.
news.summary:

news.title:
Hyundai Motor India appoints Mr. Dong Huwy Park as COO and Mr. Hyun Sup Lee as Corporate Planning Head.
Mr. Park has 30 years' experience, while Mr. Lee brings 24 years in the automotive sector, effective Jan 2026.
news.summary:

The company plans ₹45,000 Cr investment, focusing on 'Make in India' efforts.
